name: Tomato Olive Spirals
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup ripe olives, pitted and finely chopped
1/2 teaspoon dried oregano or thyme, crushed
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the hot roll mix according to package directions. After kneading the dough, divide it into thirds. Cover the dough loosely and let rest for 5 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, drain the tomatoes, reserving the oil. Chop the tomatoes. In a medium mixing bowl, combine the chopped tomatoes, cream cheese, olives, green onion, egg yolk, pepper, and oregano or thyme. If necessary, stir in enough of the reserved tomato oil (about 1 tablespoon) to achieve a filling that is easy to spread.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll each portion of dough into a rectangle measuring 14 inches by 11 inches. Spread one-third of the filling on each rectangle, leaving about half an inch from the edges. The filling amount may seem generous.
4. Roll up each rectangle tightly from a long side. Seal the seams. Place the loaves, seam side down, on a greased large baking sheet. Cover and let rise until nearly double in size, about 30 to 40 minutes.
5. Make diagonal slashes, about 1/4 inch deep, on the tops of the loaves. Beat together the egg and water and brush onto the loaves.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 25 minutes, or until the tops are golden brown. Cool on a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature. Serves 72 spirals.
To make ahead: Wrap the baked and cooled loaves individually in heavy foil and freeze. To reheat, bake each foil-wrapped roll in a preheated oven at 300°F for about 30 minutes, or until heated through. (The recipe does not specify whether to thaw the rolls first.)
